name: Village Park
desc: Small residential community within the greater Waipahu area — quiet, affordable, working-class neighborhood feel.
sources: Local RE guides · Waipahu community info
insider: It's technically part of greater Waipahu but has its own distinct community identity and neighborhood board
The neighborhood is very family-oriented — you'll see kids playing outside year-round and block parties are common
Village Park is one of the most affordable options in the Waipahu corridor — 3-bedroom homes regularly list under $800K
Proximity to both Waipahu Town Center and Waikele shopping means everything is within a 5-minute drive
The Waipahu Public Library on the edge of Village Park is well-run and has great children's programs
Street layout is simple grid-style from the 1970s — easy to navigate but the sameness can be disorienting at first
Tanioka's Seafood in nearby Waipahu is a 3-minute drive — arguably the best poke counter on the entire island
Termites are an issue in the older homes here — ask for a termite inspection report and treatment history before buying
Village Park Community Park has basketball courts and a small playground — nothing fancy but well-used by the community
The area is safe and quiet — crime stats are lower than neighboring areas, and the tight community keeps an eye on things
